mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which contracts corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer discretionary and durable goods demand, which amplifies revenue growth forecasts for S&P 500 constituents and produces positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to yield a statistically significant positive return differential with p-value greater than 0.05 over 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with near-term corporate revenue acceleration and generates positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 5-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of observations during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 235.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces markets to price in aggressive future monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0 across multiple inflation cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer expenditure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit generation, which translates into immediate positive repricing of equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO shocks are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of historical instances.
